title: lithospermum nelsonii
intro: Lithospermum nelsonii, also known as Nelson's puccoon or Nelson's lithospermum, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. It is a member of the borage family and has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Lithospermum nelsonii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal properties, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
